tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Nearly 100 Disabled Golfers Find Their Voice at Golf's Most Joyful Event
stories.summary: Nearly 96 adaptive golfers from around the world gathered at Rockville's U.S. Adaptive Open, where players like Max Togisala shot a 67 from a three-wheeled cart, proving golf's most joyful event celebrates determination over disability.

stories.headline: Community Pivots Wheelchair Fund to Give Disabled Boy His Education Back
stories.summary: When a wheelchair was donated, community members redirected remaining funds to pay school fees for a child who desperately needed to learn.

stories.headline: Tigers Roar Back from Brink: Manas Sanctuary Rebounds From 14 to Nearly 60
stories.summary: Manas National Park's tiger population roared back from just 14 in 2015 to nearly 60 today, thanks to Dr. M. Firoz Ahmed's philosophy of helping both wildlife and local communities flourish together.

stories.headline: Surgeons Guide Robots Through World-First Operation, Bringing Hope to Underserved Hospitals
stories.summary: Surgeon Shanglei Liu remotely controlled humanoid robots to perform minimally invasive gallbladder removals on live pigs, proving this affordable technology could bring advanced surgery to underserved hospitals.

stories.headline: Critically Endangered Bustard Chick Takes Flight After Surviving Vulnerable Stage
stories.summary: Overlooked athletes celebrated as champions at the U.S. Adaptive Open, where determination and community transformed the sport.

stories.headline: Seventeen-Year-Old's Tennis Ball Rescue: 5,300 Discards Find New Purpose
stories.summary: Max Shilt's nonprofit gives used tennis balls a second life in schools, shelters, and nursing homes across South Florida.

stories.headline: Meet the Robot That Swims and Flies Like a Diving Bird
stories.summary: MIT engineers Raphael Zufferey and Moritz Hüsser created a biomimetic robot inspired by 100 diving bird species that swims and flies, offering an affordable way to monitor whales and coastal ecosystems.

stories.headline: 79 Endangered Sea Turtle Hatchlings Released Into the Sea
stories.summary: Community vigilance saved 79 endangered hawksbill hatchlings spotted near 71st street in Progreso, Yucatán, when citizens alerted authorities who safely released them into the sea.

stories.headline: Bank Workers Turn Gas Cards Into Fuel for Community Care
stories.summary: Ten percent of bank workers donate personal gift cards to power Meals On Wheels delivery vehicles across their region.
